questions.q: From the following balance sheet of XYZ Ltd., prepare a cash flow statement for the year ended 31st March, 2022.
questions.answer: Prepare a cash flow statement by using direct method and indirect method.

questions.q: As on 31st March, 2022, the balance of investments in the balance sheet of ABC Ltd. appeared as follows: Equity shares of Rs. 10 each, Rs. 5,000,000 and preference shares of Rs. 100 each, Rs. 10,000,000. Calculate the return on investment of the company.
questions.answer: Return on investment = (Investment income - Investment loss) / Average investment.

questions.q: XYZ Ltd. has the following balance as on 31st December, 2022: (i) Cash Rs. 5,00,000, (ii) Bank Rs. 10,00,000, (iii) Short-term investments Rs. 15,00,000, (iv) Current liabilities Rs. 8,00,000. Prepare the statement of changes in financial position.
questions.answer: Prepare the statement of changes in financial position by using direct method and indirect method.

questions.q: From the following balance sheet of ABC Ltd., calculate the current ratio and its interpretation.
questions.answer: Current ratio = Current assets / Current liabilities

questions.q: ABC Ltd. has the following balance as on 31st March, 2022: (i) Debtors Rs. 10,00,000, (ii) Bills receivables Rs. 2,00,000, (iii) Creditors Rs. 8,00,000. Prepare the trading and profit and loss account for the year ended 31st March, 2022.
questions.answer: Prepare the trading and profit and loss account by using the given balances.

questions.q: XYZ Ltd. has the following statement of profit and loss as on 31st December, 2022: (i) Gross profit Rs. 8,00,000, (ii) Operating expenses Rs. 4,00,000, (iii) Non-operating expenses Rs. 1,00,000, (iv) Non-operating income Rs. 50,000. Calculate the net profit of the company.
questions.answer: Net profit = Gross profit + Non-operating income - Non-operating expenses.

questions.q: ABC Ltd. has the following balance sheet as on 31st March, 2022: (i) Share capital Rs. 10,00,000, (ii) Reserves and surplus Rs. 5,00,000. Calculate the proprietorship of the company.
questions.answer: Proprietorship = Share capital + Reserves and surplus.

questions.q: From the following balance sheet of XYZ Ltd., prepare a cash flow statement for the year ended 31st December, 2022.
questions.answer: Prepare a cash flow statement by using direct method and indirect method.

questions.q: The following is the statement of profit and loss of ABC Ltd. for the year ended 31st March, 2022: (i) Gross profit Rs. 10,00,000, (ii) Operating expenses Rs. 2,00,000, (iii) Non-operating expenses Rs. 50,000. Calculate the operating profit of the company.
questions.answer: Operating profit = Gross profit - Operating expenses.

questions.q: From the following balance sheet of XYZ Ltd., calculate the debt equity ratio and its interpretation.
questions.answer: Debt equity ratio = Total debt / Total equity
